macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Waller says inflation is still too hot and not moving in the right direction
items.summary: Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller said recent data show the U.S. labor market remains stable, but inflation is still too high and broad-based. He pointed to higher energy prices and noted his estimate of April PCE inflation around 3.8% year over year, well above the Fed’s 2% target.

items.title: U.S. economic calendar shows a packed day of key releases and Fed speeches
items.summary: The latest trading calendar highlights several market-moving U.S. releases, including consumer confidence, new home sales, GDP second estimate, personal income, and weekly mortgage data. It also lists speeches from Fed officials, suggesting policymakers remain in focus for traders.

items.title: Conference Board slightly raises 2026 U.S. GDP growth forecast
items.summary: The Conference Board now projects U.S. GDP growth of 1.7% year over year in 2026, up modestly from 1.6% previously. The forecast suggests continued but slower expansion as policymakers weigh sticky inflation against resilient growth.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: XRP network activity surges as new wallets jump by 4,300 in 24 hours
items.summary: XRP saw a sharp increase in on-chain activity, with 4,300 new wallets created in a single day. Despite the growth in network usage, the token’s price remained relatively unchanged.

items.title: Ethereum ETF outflows keep pressure on ETH as price risks slipping below $2,100
items.summary: Ethereum faced renewed downside pressure as ETF outflows continued to weigh on sentiment. Analysts warned ETH could fall below the $2,100 level if selling persists.

items.title: CLARITY Act advances as Senator Lummis says it would end crypto regulatory ambiguity
items.summary: U.S. crypto regulation remained in focus after Senator Cynthia Lummis said the CLARITY Act would help resolve long-standing uncertainty around digital assets. The comments underscore continued momentum for clearer federal rules.

items.title: South Korea revisits crypto tax policy after petition gathers 52,000 signatures
items.summary: South Korean lawmakers are set to review proposals that could repeal or alter the country’s crypto tax regime after a petition drew major public support. The debate reflects growing political pressure over how digital asset gains should be taxed.

items.title: China launches two-year crackdown on unlicensed offshore crypto trading
items.summary: China began a new two-year enforcement campaign targeting unlicensed offshore trading activity connected to cryptocurrencies. The move adds fresh regulatory pressure on cross-border digital asset markets.

items.title: THORChain unveils recovery plan after May 15 exploit
items.summary: THORChain proposed a recovery plan following its recent exploit and said no new RUNE was minted as part of the response. The update is aimed at restoring confidence after the security incident.

items.title: NEAR rallies as AI-linked crypto narrative regains momentum
items.summary: NEAR posted gains as traders rotated back into AI-themed crypto assets. The move suggests renewed interest in projects tied to the artificial intelligence narrative.

items.title: Cardano tops 121 million transactions as ecosystem activity continues
items.summary: Cardano crossed 121 million total transactions, highlighting continued on-chain usage even as ADA market sentiment remains challenged. The milestone points to steady network growth despite broader price weakness.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Google unveils Gemini Omni for cinematic AI video creation
items.summary: Google launched Gemini Omni, a multimodal AI model aimed at pushing beyond chatbots into full-scale video generation and conversational editing. The system can use text, images, audio, and video prompts, and Google is positioning it as a major step in AI-powered content creation.

items.title: Meta hires four more OpenAI researchers, report says
items.summary: Meta has reportedly recruited four additional OpenAI researchers, highlighting the intensifying talent war among leading AI labs. The hires underscore how aggressively major tech companies are competing for advanced model-building expertise.

items.title: OpenAI's ChatGPT experiences service issues
items.summary: Users reported problems loading prompts in ChatGPT, indicating a temporary outage or service disruption. The incident adds to growing scrutiny of reliability as AI services become more widely used.

items.title: Apple to open underlying AI technology to developers
items.summary: Apple is moving to make more of its underlying AI technology available to developers. The shift could expand third-party integration and accelerate AI features across the broader iPhone and Mac ecosystem.

items.title: AT&T and T-Mobile test AI in RAN with Ericsson without GPUs
items.summary: AT&T and T-Mobile are showing AI use in radio access networks using Ericsson's AI-native link adaptation feature, aimed at improving spectrum efficiency and network performance without GPUs. The development suggests telecom operators are increasingly exploring lightweight, embedded AI at the network edge.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: No relevant video game or gaming industry news found in the provided search results
items.summary: The supplied results are mostly YouTube videos and unrelated items, with no clear verified coverage of major game releases, announcements, esports, or industry developments from the past 24 hours.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Samsung to livestream K-pop concerts monthly on its TVs for free
items.summary: Samsung said it will begin offering free monthly livestreams of K-pop music concerts on its TVs, expanding its entertainment content strategy. The move highlights how TV makers are competing on services and not just hardware.

items.title: Meta quietly launches dedicated Facebook Groups app, Forum
items.summary: Meta has reportedly released a separate app for Facebook Groups called Forum, signaling renewed focus on community features. The launch appears to have been done with little publicity.

items.title: Ayaneo gives first official look at Game Boy-like Pocket Block
items.summary: Ayaneo has revealed its Pocket Block handheld, a retro-styled gaming device resembling the classic Game Boy. The announcement adds to the company’s expanding lineup of niche gaming hardware.

items.title: HMD bundles Indian AI chatbot onto new smartphone to target local market
items.summary: Finnish phone maker HMD is pairing an Indian AI chatbot with a new smartphone as it seeks to win over customers in India. The strategy reflects the growing importance of localized software partnerships in mobile hardware.

items.title: Google replaces another major piece of Fitbit’s identity
items.summary: Google is continuing to phase out Fitbit branding and features as part of its broader integration of the wearable company into its own ecosystem. The latest change is another sign that Fitbit is losing its standalone identity.

items.title: Google announces AI-powered Ask YouTube search feature
items.summary: Google has introduced an AI-powered Ask YouTube search feature designed to help users find videos and answers more directly. The rollout adds another layer of machine-learning tools to YouTube’s search experience.

items.title: Microsoft continues Copilot rollback as Office lets users move button to the ribbon
items.summary: Microsoft is further adjusting Copilot’s placement in Office apps, allowing users to move its button to the ribbon instead of keeping it prominent in the interface. The change suggests ongoing refinement after user pushback.

items.title: Apple releases Safari Technology Preview 244 with bug fixes and performance improvements
items.summary: Apple has issued Safari Technology Preview 244, bringing bug fixes and performance upgrades for developers testing upcoming browser changes. The release is part of Apple’s regular pre-production Safari update cycle.

items.title: Oura files confidentially for U.S. initial public offering
items.summary: Smart ring maker Oura has confidentially filed for a U.S. IPO, marking a major corporate milestone for the wearable company. The move could set up one of the more notable consumer hardware listings in the sector.

items.title: Anker launches earbuds with AI chip for stronger noise reduction
items.summary: Anker has unveiled new earbuds featuring its AI chip, which the company says improves noise reduction performance. The launch underscores continued competition in premium wireless audio hardware.
